page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.6xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, utilizing the Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at a baseline frequency of 3.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage solutions for data persistence. It also lacks integrated GPU hardware accelerators and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. For network connectivity, the instance offers a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

The dedicated core allocation without hyperthreading provides predictable compute performance, making it a stable option for workloads requiring consistent CPU execution. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a general-purpose resource. It is suitable for application servers, containerized microservices, web serving, and moderate database workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9i.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores operating at 3.2 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, providing predictable compute performance without hyperthreading. The system is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local storage and GPU accelerators, meaning all storage must be attached externally. This hardware profile is designed for workloads such as web serving, application hosting, and containerized deployments that require stable, dedicated CPU resources and a standard memory-to-core ratio.
